# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Define the directory where page templates are stored
define('TEMPLATES_DIR', __DIR__ . '/templates/');

// --- Configuration for Allowed Pages (Security) ---
// This array defines which pages are allowed to be loaded.
// It maps a "friendly name" from the URL to the actual filename.
// This prevents arbitrary file inclusion vulnerabilities.
$allowedPages = [
    'home' => 'home.php',
    'about' => 'about.php',
    'contact' => 'contact.php',
    'products' => 'products.php',
    'services' => 'services.php',
    // Add more pages as needed:
    // 'url_name' => 'filename.php',
];

// Get the requested page from the URL, default to 'home' if not set
$requestedPage = $_GET['page'] ?? 'home';

// Initialize variables for the file to include and the page title
$pageToInclude = '';
$pageTitle = 'My Website'; // Default title

// --- Router Logic ---
if (array_key_exists($requestedPage, $allowedPages)) {
    // If the requested page is in our allowed list, build the full path
    $pageToInclude = TEMPLATES_DIR . $allowedPages[$requestedPage];
    // Set a more specific title for the page
    $pageTitle = ucfirst($requestedPage) . ' - My Website';
} else {
    // If the page is not allowed or doesn't exist, set it to a 404 page
    // You should create a '404.php' file in your templates directory
    $pageToInclude = TEMPLATES_DIR . '404.php';
    $pageTitle = 'Page Not Found - My Website';
    // Optionally send a 404 HTTP status code
    header("HTTP/1.0 404 Not Found");
}

// --- Page Structure (Layout) ---
// This is the main layout of your website.
// It includes common elements like header, navigation, and footer,
// and then injects the specific page content.
?>
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title><?php echo htmlspecialchars($pageTitle); ?></title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/css/style.css">
    <!-- You might want to include other meta tags, favicon, etc. -->
</head>
<body>
    <header>
        <h1>My Awesome CMS</h1>
        <nav>
            <ul>
                <li><a href="?page=home">Home</a></li>
                <li><a href="?page=about">About Us</a></li>
                <li><a href="?page=services">Services</a></li>
                <li><a href="?page=products">Products</a></li>
                <li><a href="?page=contact">Contact</a></li>
                <!-- Example of a non-existent page for testing 404 -->
                <li><a href="?page=nonexistent">Non-existent Page</a></li>
            </ul>
        </nav>
    </header>

    <main>
        <?php
        // --- Include the requested page content ---
        // Ensure the file exists before including it to prevent errors
        if (file_exists($pageToInclude)) {
            include $pageToInclude;
        } else {
            // This fallback should ideally not be reached if allowedPages is set up correctly
            // and 404.php exists, but it's a good safety net.
            echo '<div style="color: red;">Error: Template file not found on server for path: ' . htmlspecialchars($pageToInclude) . '</div>';
        }
        ?>
    </main>

    <footer>
        <p>&copy; <?php echo date('Y'); ?> My Company. All rights reserved.</p>
    </footer>

    <script src="/assets/js/script.js"></script>
    <!-- Any other global scripts -->
</body>
</html>
<?php
// Example content for your /templates/ directory:

// --- templates/home.php ---
// <h1>Welcome to our Homepage!</h1>
// <p>This is the main content for the home page.</p>

// --- templates/about.php ---
// <h1>About Us</h1>
// <p>Learn more about our company and our mission.</p>

// --- templates/contact.php ---
// <h1>Contact Us</h1>
// <p>Get in touch with us through this page.</p>
// <form>...</form>

// --- templates/products.php ---
// <h1>Our Products</h1>
// <p>Explore our range of amazing products.</p>

// --- templates/services.php ---
// <h1>Our Services</h1>
// <p>Discover the services we offer.</p>

// --- templates/404.php ---
// <h1>404 - Page Not Found</h1>
// <p>Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist.</p>
// <p><a href="?page=home">Go back to the homepage</a></p>

// --- Directory Structure ---
//
// your_project/
// ├── index.php         (This file)
// ├── templates/
// │   ├── home.php
// │   ├── about.php
// │   ├── contact.php
// │   ├── products.php
// │   ├── services.php
// │   └── 404.php
// ├── assets/
// │   ├── css/
// │   │   └── style.css
// │   └── js/
// │       └── script.js
// └── .htaccess         (Optional, for URL rewriting to hide index.php)
//
// --- Example .htaccess for clean URLs (optional) ---
// This would allow URLs like /home or /about instead of ?page=home
//
// RewriteEngine On
// RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
// RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d
// RewriteRule ^(.*)$ index.php?page=$1 [L,QSA]
//
// With .htaccess, you would change `href="?page=home"` to `href="/home"`
// and modify the router logic to parse the REQUEST_URI if needed,
// but for a simple GET parameter, it's not strictly necessary.
?>
